_meta.description: Evening Prokeimena for Vespers — weekday, great feast, and special occasions

saturdayGreatVespers.label: Great Prokeimenon
saturdayGreatVespers.note: Sung at Saturday Great Vespers in place of the weekday prokeimenon. Always Tone 8.
saturdayGreatVespers.refrain: Thou hast given an inheritance to them that fear Thee, O Lord.

verses.text: From the ends of the earth I call to Thee.
I will take shelter under the shadow of Thy wings.
So I will ever sing praises to Thy Name.

saturday.label: The Lord is King
saturday.refrain: The Lord is King: He is robed in majesty.

verses.text: The Lord is robed, He is girded with strength.
For He has established the world, so that it shall never be moved.
Holiness befits Thy house, O Lord, forevermore!

sunday.label: Behold now, bless the Lord
sunday.refrain: Behold now, bless the Lord, all you servants of the Lord.

verses.text: You that stand in the house of the Lord, even in the courts of the house of our God.

monday.label: The Lord will hear me
monday.refrain: The Lord will hear me when I call upon Him.

verses.text: When I called, the God of my righteousness heard me.

tuesday.label: Thy mercy shall follow me
tuesday.refrain: Thy mercy, O Lord, shall follow me all the days of my life.

verses.text: The Lord is my shepherd; therefore can I lack nothing: He maketh me to lie down in a green pasture.

wednesday.label: Save me, O God
wednesday.refrain: Save me, O God, by Thy Name, and judge me by Thy strength.

verses.text: Hear my prayer, O God: hearken unto the words of my mouth.

thursday.label: My help comes from the Lord
thursday.refrain: My help comes from the Lord Who made heaven and earth.

verses.text: I lifted up mine eyes unto the hills: from whence will my help come?

friday.label: Thou art my helper
friday.refrain: Thou, O God, art my helper, and Thy mercy shall go before me.

verses.text: Rescue me from mine enemies, O God: and deliver me from them that rise up against me.

holyFriday._note: Prokeimenon for Great and Holy Friday Burial Vespers. Source: OCA Triodion.
holyFriday.label: They parted my garments
holyFriday.refrain: They parted my garments among them, and upon my vesture did they cast lots.

verses.text: My God, my God, look upon me; why hast Thou forsaken me?

whoIsSoGreat._note: Sung on the evenings of 25 December and Thomas Sunday and at Kneeling Vespers of Holy Pentecost. Exception: if Christmas falls on Saturday, the usual Saturday prokeimenon is used.
whoIsSoGreat.label: Who is so great a God
whoIsSoGreat.refrain: Who is so great a god as our God? Thou art the God Who doest wonders.

verses.text: Thou hast made known Thy power among the peoples.
And I said, 'Now have I begun; this is the change of the right hand of the Most High.'
I remembered the works of the Lord, for from the beginning will I remember Thy wonders.

ourGodIsInHeaven._note: Sung on the evenings of 14 September, 6 January, Holy Ascension, and 6 August. Exception: if these fall on Saturday, the usual Saturday prokeimenon is used and this is sung the previous evening.
ourGodIsInHeaven.label: Our God is in heaven
ourGodIsInHeaven.refrain: Our God is in heaven and on earth: He hath done whatsoever He hath pleased.

verses.text: When Israel came out of Egypt, the house of Jacob from among a strange people;
The sea saw it and fled: Jordan was driven back.
What aileth thee, O thou sea, that thou fleddest? thou Jordan, that thou wast driven back?

soulSaturday._note: Used on the eve of Soul Saturdays instead of the prokeimenon

verses.text: Blessed are they whom Thou hast chosen and taken to Thyself, O Lord.
Their memory is from generation to generation.

lenten._note: Prokeimenon pairs before Genesis and Proverbs readings at Lenten weekday Vespers. Nested as week.N.dow.{genesis,proverbs}. Source: OCA service texts (TT).

genesis.refrain: Thou, O Lord, shalt protect us / and preserve us from this generation forever.

verses.text: Save me, O Lord, for there is no longer any that is godly! (Ps. 12:1a)

proverbs.refrain: Consider and answer me, / O Lord my God!

verses.text: How long, O Lord, wilt Thou forget me forever? How long wilt Thou hide Thy face from me? (Ps. 12:1)

genesis.refrain: Be glad in the Lord, / and rejoice, O you righteous!

verses.text: Blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven. (Ps. 31:1a)

proverbs.refrain: Let Thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us / as we have set our hope on Thee!

verses.text: Rejoice in the Lord, O you righteous! Praise befits the just! (Ps. 32:1)

genesis.refrain: I will trust in the mercy of God / for ever and ever.

verses.text: Why dost thou boast, O mighty man, of mischief done against the godly? (Ps. 51:1)

proverbs.refrain: When the Lord turns back / the captivity of His people.

verses.text: The fool says in his heart, “There is no God.” (Ps. 13:1)

genesis.refrain: Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, / Who alone does wondrous things!

verses.text: Give the king Thy justice, O God, and Thy righteousness to the royal son! (Ps. 71:1)

proverbs.refrain: It is good for me / to be near my God.

verses.text: Truly God is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart. (Ps. 72:1)

genesis.refrain: Guide me in Thy way, O Lord, / that I may walk in Thy truth!

verses.text: Incline Thine ear, O Lord, and hear me! (Ps. 85:1a)

proverbs.refrain: The Lord loves the gates of Zion / more than all the dwellings of Jacob.

verses.text: His foundations are on the holy mountains. (Ps. 86:1a)

genesis.refrain: I will walk before the Lord / in the land of the living.

verses.text: I love the Lord because He has heard my voice and my supplication. (Ps. 114:1)

proverbs.refrain: I will offer my prayers to the Lord / in the presence of all His people.

verses.text: I kept my faith, even when I said: “I am greatly afflicted.” (Ps. 115:1)
